title: Love and Sex in Evangelion Escapism or Something More
display_title: Love and Sex in Evangelion Escapism or Something More
wikitext:
[]: Love & Sex in Evangelion: Escapism, or Something More?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 05, 2014 2:48 am ChaddyManPrime wrote: I mean holy shit, I don't think anyone has sex in this show just because they love each other, it's always something inherently fucked up.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Jul 05, 2014 3:19 am Nope. Misato and Kaji had shitlods of their own mental crap and sex was awkward painkiller for them as well as later a mean for Kaji to pass information. It was a tool for purpose, not expression of love.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 05, 2014 3:24 am Stillborn wrote: Nope. Misato and Kaji had shitlods of their own mental crap and sex was awkward painkiller for them as well as later a mean for Kaji to pass information. It was a tool for purpose, not expression of love.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Jul 05, 2014 4:22 am For reasons I mentioned. Emotional painkiller.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 05, 2014 4:26 am Stillborn wrote: For reasons I mentioned. Emotional painkiller.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Jul 05, 2014 4:46 am Fine, I'll stop argue now since you are apparently an expert on "how it works" for emotionally damaged people, and I can not compete with your overwhelming experience in this field.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jul 05, 2014 5:53 am Call me crazy for agreeing with Stillborn, but I have trouble seeing Misato and Kaji's week of sex as anything but Misato binging on sex as an escape. It was certainly not portrayed in a positive light and seemed to be used within the story to indicate a problem Misato had. If it had been any other pleasurable activity -- say, video games -- I think anyone would have agreed that it was clearly unhealthy and representative of a deeper problem.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Sat Jul 05, 2014 7:58 am Thirded that opinion. It was clearly escapism, as far as I can tell.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaBrothers » Sat Jul 05, 2014 8:03 am Monk Ed wrote: (...) anything but Misato binging on sex as an escape. It was certainly not portrayed in a positive light (...)

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Jul 05, 2014 8:16 am There is no love in sex in Eva. That would mean shipping and Anno hates shipping in his Eva. Additionally there's no love at all in Eva, romantic or otherwise. Every notable relationship is one character using the other as a short term band aid for their own pains, disregarding the other person's position. Everyone is selfish and egoistic looking for a substitute for something or someone and use others as said substitutes.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sat Jul 05, 2014 8:32 am ^ If Anno believed ANY of that tripe you're spilling, why is the message of EoE to learn to love yourself, which can help you love others? And further more, if he's so AGAINST shipping of any kind and against love in his work, why the hell is he happily married? Hmmm? He's shipping himself with his wife. Seriously Stillborn, enough with this whole pessimistic/nihilistic rants you keep raving on regarding EVERYTHING to do with Eva. If you don't like it, DON'T WATCH IT. It's that simple.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Jul 05, 2014 8:42 am If you don't like my posts don't read them. It's that simple. But you still reading them, even if they piss you off. It's not that easy to turn away from Eva, even if it's like nails on chalkboard. And by the time Anno made Eva he wasn't married. He was complaining about otakus. He is married now, that why in NTE only Shinji is blatant egoist instead of everyone and their pinguin. End Eva's message to love yourself? Even if it's true, It does not contradict what I say: Love yourself and stop using others as substitute.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaBrothers » Sat Jul 05, 2014 8:52 am Stillborn wrote: Love yourself and stop using others as substitute.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sat Jul 05, 2014 9:05 am Stillborn wrote: Even if it's true, It does not contradict what I say: Love yourself and stop using others as substitute.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Jul 05, 2014 10:58 am Sooo... You're a self appointed, lonely crusader that has to protect feeble minds of other users, from evil that I represent, since they don't know any better? Misato can't decide if she wants to see Shinji as her son, brother or boyfriend. He is just there to fill certain void left by her father and lack of Kaji. And by the EoE she gives him an "adult kiss". Something that is neither motherly nor sisterly, but even that is not out of love but to put certain preassure on him. By the EoE nothing changed in their relationship. She still doesnt know what Shinji is suppose to be to her. There is attachment from both, but no love.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jul 05, 2014 11:02 am Stillborn wrote: Sooo... You're a self appointed, lonely crusader

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Jul 05, 2014 11:07 am Aww guys, don't pick on the Good Sir He can't help the way he feels, if he can only see the bleakness then he can only see the bleakness.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 05, 2014 11:23 am xanderkh wrote: And further more, if he's so AGAINST shipping of any kind and against love in his work, why the hell is he happily married? Hmmm? He's shipping himself with his wife.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaBrothers » Sat Jul 05, 2014 12:17 pm I think love in Evangelion is not absent, only buried under a heavy load of other psychological issues that won't let it come up to surface. Kaji and Misato do love each other, but they can't find a place for happiness together in their life.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 05, 2014 1:25 pm EvaBrothers wrote: I think love in Evangelion is not absent, only buried under a heavy load of other psychological issues that won't let it come up to surface. Kaji and Misato do love each other, but they can't find a place for happiness together in their life.
